Community Dynamics

Nick Chang

2025-02-26

Reading in data

#pointsPlots = read.csv('../data/pointsPlots.csv')

points = read.csv("../data/points_2025-01-29.csv")

plots = read.csv("../data/plots_2025-01-29.csv")

pointsWide = read.csv("../data/pointsWide_2024-12-22.csv")

bands = read.csv("../data/bands_2025-01-29.csv")

bandRocks = read.csv("../data/MicrohabAvail_2025-02-20.csv")


cols = c('CHOW' = '#0c9896', 
        'PSRUB' = "#C95945",
         "ECIR" = "#E6BC52",
         'EGUT' = '#E68E1C', 
         "DCHE" = "#5D695C", 
          #"DCHE" = "#70665e",
          "DPER" = "#81705c",
          #'DPER' = '#AFA060', 
         'CAM' = "#c7a891"
         )

habCols = c("stream" = "#9BC4BC", "margin" = "#4B5043", "terrestrial" = "#598218")

Reading out a breakdown of how many escaped, NOID desmognathus there were:

points %>% group_by(Species, Escaped) %>% summarize(n())
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Species'. You can override using the
## `.groups` argument.
## # A tibble: 16 × 3
## # Groups:   Species [10]
##    Species Escaped `n()`
##    <chr>   <chr>   <int>
##  1 CAM     NO         52
##  2 CAM     YES         4
##  3 CHOW    NO          8
##  4 D       NO          1
##  5 D       YES         3
##  6 DCHE    NO         24
##  7 DCHE    YES        10
##  8 DPER    NO         56
##  9 DPER    YES        22
## 10 ECIR    NO         23
## 11 ECIR    YES        11
## 12 EGUT    NO          1
## 13 PSRUB   NO          4
## 14 PSRUB   YES         2
## 15 SAL     YES         1
## 16 <NA>    <NA>      186
points %>% filter(Species %in% c("D", "SAL")) %>% select(Escaped, Species)
##   Escaped Species
## 1     YES       D
## 2     YES       D
## 3     YES     SAL
## 4     YES       D
## 5      NO       D
pointsWide %>% filter(DCHE > 1) %>% select(CoverType, DCHE)
##   CoverType DCHE
## 1         R    2
## 2         R    2

Creating a plot to show the size class distribution of D perlapsus based on whether they were in streams with seal salamanders

points %>%
  filter(Species %in% c('DPER')) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = mmSVL)) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(fill = as.factor(sealStream)), alpha = 0.5, position = "identity", bins = 20) + 
 # facet_wrap(~sealStream.x) + 
  theme_bw()
## Warning: Removed 19 rows containing non-finite values (`stat_bin()`).

Stream distance/cover position

#creating the cover item position variable (stream/margin/terr)
points = points  %>% 
  mutate(hab = ifelse(distWater > 50, "terrestrial", NA)) %>%
  mutate(hab = ifelse(cmDepth > 1, "stream", hab)) %>%
  mutate(hab = ifelse(is.na(hab) == T, "margin", hab)) %>%
  mutate(hab = as.factor(hab)) %>%
  mutate(distDepth = distWater - cmDepth)
# I am going to create a color palette to make this easier to interpret

Do seals utilize more aquatic habitat than other species

points %>% filter(sealStream==1) %>% 
  mutate(Species = ifelse(Species == "CHOW", "CAM", Species)) %>% 
    filter(Species %in% c("CAM", "DCHE", "DPER", "ECIR")) %>% group_by(Species) %>% summarize(count = n())
## # A tibble: 4 × 2
##   Species count
##   <chr>   <int>
## 1 CAM        38
## 2 DCHE       34
## 3 DPER       49
## 4 ECIR       20
pointsSealStream = points %>% filter(sealStream == 1) %>% filter(hab != "terrestrial") %>% 
  mutate(Species = ifelse(Species == "CHOW", "CAM", Species)) %>% filter(Species %in% c("CAM", "DCHE", "DPER", "ECIR")) %>% 
  mutate(Species = ifelse(Species == "CAM", "xCAM", Species))

pointsSealStream %>% group_by(Species) %>% summarize(count = n())
## # A tibble: 4 × 2
##   Species count
##   <chr>   <int>
## 1 DCHE       34
## 2 DPER       45
## 3 ECIR       20
## 4 xCAM       38
pointsSealStream = pointsSealStream %>% mutate(habBin = ifelse(hab == "stream", 1, 0))

nullComp = compHab = glmmTMB(habBin ~ (1|StrmID), data = pointsSealStream, family = "binomial")


compHab = glmmTMB(habBin ~ Species + (1|StrmID), data = pointsSealStream, family = "binomial")
summary(compHab)
##  Family: binomial  ( logit )
## Formula:          habBin ~ Species + (1 | StrmID)
## Data: pointsSealStream
## 
##      AIC      BIC   logLik deviance df.resid 
##    154.4    169.0    -72.2    144.4      132 
## 
## Random effects:
## 
## Conditional model:
##  Groups Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  StrmID (Intercept) 0.7094   0.8423  
## Number of obs: 137, groups:  StrmID, 7
## 
## Conditional model:
##             Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)   
## (Intercept)   0.7538     0.5254   1.435  0.15133   
## SpeciesDPER  -1.5563     0.5479  -2.840  0.00451 **
## SpeciesECIR  -0.5929     0.6379  -0.930  0.35259   
## SpeciesxCAM   1.3871     0.7935   1.748  0.08045 . 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
tab_model(compHab)
  habBin
Predictors Odds Ratios CI p
(Intercept) 2.13 0.76 – 5.95 0.151
Species [DPER] 0.21 0.07 – 0.62 0.005
Species [ECIR] 0.55 0.16 – 1.93 0.353
Species [xCAM] 4.00 0.85 – 18.96 0.080
Random Effects
σ2 3.29
τ00 StrmID 0.71
ICC 0.18
N StrmID 7
Observations 137
Marginal R2 / Conditional R2 0.252 / 0.384 
anova(nullComp, compHab, test = "Chisq")
## Data: pointsSealStream
## Models:
## nullComp: habBin ~ (1 | StrmID), zi=~0, disp=~1
## compHab: habBin ~ Species + (1 | StrmID), zi=~0, disp=~1
##          Df    AIC    BIC  logLik deviance  Chisq Chi Df Pr(>Chisq)    
## nullComp  2 169.87 175.71 -82.936   165.87                             
## compHab   5 154.43 169.03 -72.217   144.43 21.438      3  8.538e-05 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
exp(-0.32)
## [1] 0.726149

visualization

dist = pointsSealStream %>%
  group_by(Species) %>%
  mutate(spCount = n()) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(Species, hab) %>%
  mutate(habProp = n() / spCount) %>%
  ungroup()


FIG12 = dist %>% filter(Species %in% c("CAM", "DCHE", "DPER", "ECIR")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Species, y = habProp, fill = factor(hab, levels = c("stream", "margin", "terrestrial")))) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "fill",
           aes(col = factor(hab, levels = c("stream", "margin", "terrestrial"))
               )) + 
  scale_fill_manual(values = habCols) + 
    scale_color_manual(values = habCols) +
  guides(color="none") + 
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  geom_text(aes(y = 1.05, label = paste("n = ", spCount))) + 
  labs(fill = "") + 
  theme_minimal() +
  ylab("Proportion of Observed Animals") + 
  #title("Proportions of Commonly Observed Species Observed in Different Habitat Types") + 
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) + theme( text = element_text(size = 12, family = "Times"))

FIG12

Is the proportion of duskies using stream/marginal habitat different depending on whether there are seals in the stream?

points %>% filter(Species == "DPER") %>% 
  ggplot() + 
  geom_bar(aes(x = hab)) + 
  facet_wrap(~sealStream)

dper = points %>% filter(Species %in% c("DPER")) %>%
  group_by(sealStream, Species) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(sSCount = dplyr::n()) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  group_by(sealStream, hab, Species) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(habProp = dplyr::n() / sSCount) %>%
  ungroup()

FIGURE 11: STACKED BARPLOT SHOWING DISTRIBUTION OF D PERLAPSUS

dper2 = dper %>% filter(hab %in% c("stream", "margin"))

fig11 = dper2 %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = as.factor(sealStream), y = habProp, fill = factor(hab, levels = c("stream", "margin", "terrestrial")))) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "fill",
           aes(col = factor(hab, levels = c("stream", "margin", "terrestrial"))
               )) + 
  scale_fill_manual(values = habCols) + 
    scale_color_manual(values = habCols) +
  guides(color="none") + 
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  geom_text(aes(y = 1.05, label = paste("n = ", sSCount))) + 
  labs(fill = ""#, 
      # title = expression(paste("Cover Item Positions of ", italic("Desmognathus perlapsus")))
       ) + 
  theme_minimal() +
  ylab("Proportion of Observed Animals") + 
  xlab("Detection of Seal Salamanders in the Sampled Stream") + 
  theme(panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), 
    text = element_text(size = 20, family = "Times")
  ) + 
  coord_flip()

fig11

Dusky stream habitat use

First, I need to clean data

I need to add reach-level

#bandRocks = read_excel("../data/PlotData_Spring2023.xlsx", sheet = "MicrohabitatAvailability2")

are there differing counts of rocks across streams?

rockMod = lm(rockCount ~ StrmID, data = bandRocks)


summary(rockMod)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = rockCount ~ StrmID, data = bandRocks)
## 
## Residuals:
##    Min     1Q Median     3Q    Max 
## -17.60  -4.80   0.60   4.85  21.40 
## 
## Coefficients:
##              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)    15.800      2.533   6.239  1.1e-08 ***
## StrmIDSTRM02   -2.800      3.582  -0.782 0.436214    
## StrmIDSTRM03   11.900      3.582   3.323 0.001251 ** 
## StrmIDSTRM04   -4.500      3.582  -1.256 0.211920    
## StrmIDSTRM05   -9.000      3.582  -2.513 0.013591 *  
## StrmIDSTRM06    6.400      3.582   1.787 0.077013 .  
## StrmIDSTRM07    5.300      3.582   1.480 0.142106    
## StrmIDSTRM08   14.300      3.582   3.993 0.000126 ***
## StrmIDSTRM09    0.700      3.582   0.195 0.845447    
## StrmIDSTRM10    4.500      3.582   1.256 0.211920    
## StrmIDSTRM11    6.800      3.582   1.899 0.060530 .  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 8.009 on 99 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.4349, Adjusted R-squared:  0.3778 
## F-statistic: 7.618 on 10 and 99 DF,  p-value: 6.524e-09
x = Anova(rockMod)

summary(x)
##      Sum Sq           Df           F value          Pr(>F) 
##  Min.   :4886   Min.   :10.00   Min.   :7.618   Min.   :0  
##  1st Qu.:5252   1st Qu.:32.25   1st Qu.:7.618   1st Qu.:0  
##  Median :5618   Median :54.50   Median :7.618   Median :0  
##  Mean   :5618   Mean   :54.50   Mean   :7.618   Mean   :0  
##  3rd Qu.:5984   3rd Qu.:76.75   3rd Qu.:7.618   3rd Qu.:0  
##  Max.   :6350   Max.   :99.00   Max.   :7.618   Max.   :0  
##                                 NA's   :1       NA's   :1
bandRocks = bandRocks %>% mutate(SubplotHab = paste(SubplotCode, HabitatType)) %>% select(SubplotHab, rockCount)

dper = dper %>% separate(SubplotCode, into = c("Stream2", "plot", "band"), sep = "-")

dper = dper %>% mutate(SubplotHab = paste0(StrmID, "-", band, " ", toupper(hab)))

dper = dper %>% filter(hab %in% c("margin", "stream")) %>% left_join(bandRocks, by = "SubplotHab")
## Warning in left_join(., bandRocks, by = "SubplotHab"): Detected an unexpected many-to-many relationship between `x` and `y`.
## ℹ Row 30 of `x` matches multiple rows in `y`.
## ℹ Row 76 of `y` matches multiple rows in `x`.
## ℹ If a many-to-many relationship is expected, set `relationship =
##   "many-to-many"` to silence this warning.
dper = dper %>% filter(duplicated(PointCode) == FALSE)
dper2 = dper %>% filter(hab %in% c("stream", "margin") & CoverType %in% c("R", "W")) %>%
  mutate(hab2 = ifelse(hab == "stream", 1, 0))

dper2 %>% ggplot() + 
  geom_bar(aes(x = CoverType))

glimpse(dper2)
## Rows: 63
## Columns: 39
## $ X            <int> 7, 9, 13, 15, 18, 32, 34, 35, 36, 38, 44, 45, 48, 52, 55,…
## $ PointCode    <chr> "STRM01-OCC-006", "STRM01-OCC-008", "STRM01-OCC-010", "ST…
## $ StrmID       <chr> "STRM01", "STRM01", "STRM01", "STRM01", "STRM01", "STRM02…
## $ Date         <chr> "2023-03-17", "2023-03-17", "2023-03-17", "2023-03-17", "…
## $ Stream2      <chr> "STRM01", "STRM01", "STRM01", "STRM01", "STRM01", "STRM02…
## $ plot         <chr> "1", "1", "1", "1", "1", "3", "3", "3", "3", "3", "3", "3…
## $ band         <chr> "3", "3", "4", "5", "5", "1", "1", "1", "1", "1", "3", "3…

## $ NN_occ_cm    <int> 82, 15, 9, 95, 95, 54, 27, 27, 100, 60, 20, 19, 70, 46, 9…
## $ NN_vac_cm    <int> 38, 12, 18, 41, 12, 6, 8, 20, 12, 11, 12, 14, 23, 16, 36,…

Testing whether habitat utilization is dependent on the presence of D. cheaha in the stream

dperNull = glmmTMB(hab2 ~ (1|StrmID), data = dper2, family = "binomial")

dperMod = glmmTMB(hab2 ~ sealStream + rockCount + (1|StrmID), data = dper2, family = "binomial")

dperSimpl = glmmTMB(hab2 ~ sealStream + (1|StrmID), data = dper2, family = "binomial")

AICc(dperMod)
## [1] 88.75139
AICc(dperSimpl)
## [1] 86.46875
anova(dperMod, dperNull, test = "Chisq")
## Data: dper2
## Models:
## dperNull: hab2 ~ (1 | StrmID), zi=~0, disp=~1
## dperMod: hab2 ~ sealStream + rockCount + (1 | StrmID), zi=~0, disp=~1
##          Df    AIC    BIC  logLik deviance  Chisq Chi Df Pr(>Chisq)  
## dperNull  2 89.023 93.310 -42.512   85.023                           
## dperMod   4 88.062 96.634 -40.031   80.062 4.9615      2    0.08368 .
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
anova(dperMod, dperSimpl, test = "Chisq")
## Data: dper2
## Models:
## dperSimpl: hab2 ~ sealStream + (1 | StrmID), zi=~0, disp=~1
## dperMod: hab2 ~ sealStream + rockCount + (1 | StrmID), zi=~0, disp=~1
##           Df    AIC    BIC  logLik deviance Chisq Chi Df Pr(>Chisq)
## dperSimpl  3 86.062 92.491 -40.031   80.062                        
## dperMod    4 88.062 96.634 -40.031   80.062 2e-04      1     0.9876
  summary(dperMod)
##  Family: binomial  ( logit )
## Formula:          hab2 ~ sealStream + rockCount + (1 | StrmID)

## 
##      AIC      BIC   logLik deviance df.resid

## 
## Random effects:
## 
## Conditional model:

##  StrmID (Intercept) 4.493e-09 6.703e-05
## Number of obs: 63, groups:  StrmID, 10
## 
## Conditional model:
##               Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)  
## (Intercept)  0.5457640  0.9971663   0.547   0.5842  
## sealStream  -1.3216773  0.6488751  -2.037   0.0417 *
## rockCount    0.0004816  0.0310641   0.016   0.9876  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
summary(dperSimpl)

Manually calculating marginal and conditional R2

# mF = glmer(hab2 ~ sealStream + covDens + (1|PlotCode/bandNum), data = dper3, family = "binomial")


# r.squaredGLMM(mF)
# 
# 
# 
# summary(mF)
summary(dperMod)
##  Family: binomial  ( logit )
## Formula:          hab2 ~ sealStream + rockCount + (1 | StrmID)
## Data: dper2
## 
##      AIC      BIC   logLik deviance df.resid 
##     88.1     96.6    -40.0     80.1       59 
## 
## Random effects:
## 
## Conditional model:
##  Groups Name        Variance  Std.Dev. 
##  StrmID (Intercept) 4.493e-09 6.703e-05
## Number of obs: 63, groups:  StrmID, 10
## 
## Conditional model:
##               Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)  
## (Intercept)  0.5457640  0.9971663   0.547   0.5842  
## sealStream  -1.3216773  0.6488751  -2.037   0.0417 *
## rockCount    0.0004816  0.0310641   0.016   0.9876  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
# Extraction of fitted value for the alternative model
# fixef() extracts coefficents for fixed effects
# mF@X returns fixed effect design matrix
# Fixed <- fixef(mF)[2] * mF@X[, 2] + fixef(mF)[3] * mF@X[, 3] + fixef(mF)[4] * mF@X[, 4]
# 
# # Calculation of the variance in fitted values
# VarF <- var(Fixed)
# 
# # An alternative way for getting the same result
# VarF <- var(as.vector(fixef(mF) %*% t(mF@X)))
summary(dperMod)
##  Family: binomial  ( logit )
## Formula:          hab2 ~ sealStream + rockCount + (1 | StrmID)
## Data: dper2
## 
##      AIC      BIC   logLik deviance df.resid 
##     88.1     96.6    -40.0     80.1       59 
## 
## Random effects:
## 
## Conditional model:
##  Groups Name        Variance  Std.Dev. 
##  StrmID (Intercept) 4.493e-09 6.703e-05
## Number of obs: 63, groups:  StrmID, 10
## 
## Conditional model:
##               Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)  
## (Intercept)  0.5457640  0.9971663   0.547   0.5842  
## sealStream  -1.3216773  0.6488751  -2.037   0.0417 *
## rockCount    0.0004816  0.0310641   0.016   0.9876  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
tab_model(dperMod)
  hab2
Predictors Odds Ratios CI p
(Intercept) 1.73 0.24 – 12.18 0.584
sealStream 0.27 0.07 – 0.95 0.042
rockCount 1.00 0.94 – 1.06 0.988
Random Effects
σ2 3.29
τ00 StrmID 0.00
N StrmID 10
Observations 63
Marginal R2 / Conditional R2 0.110 / NA 
confint(dperMod)
##                                  2.5 %      97.5 %      Estimate
## (Intercept)                -1.40864601  2.50017394  5.457640e-01
## sealStream                 -2.59344917 -0.04990552 -1.321677e+00
## rockCount                  -0.06040287  0.06136598  4.815583e-04
## Std.Dev.(Intercept)|StrmID  0.00000000         Inf  6.703074e-05
r2(dperMod)
## Warning: Can't compute random effect variances. Some variance components equal
##   zero. Your model may suffer from singularity (see `?lme4::isSingular`
##   and `?performance::check_singularity`).
##   Solution: Respecify random structure! You may also decrease the
##   `tolerance` level to enforce the calculation of random effect variances.
## Random effect variances not available. Returned R2 does not account for random effects.
## # R2 for Mixed Models
## 
##   Conditional R2: NA

check_singularity(dperMod)
## [1] TRUE

Morphometrics

Sample Size Overview

points %>% filter(Occupied == 1) %>%
  group_by(Species, Escaped) %>%
  summarize(n())
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Species'. You can override using the
## `.groups` argument.
## # A tibble: 15 × 3
## # Groups:   Species [9]
##    Species Escaped `n()`
##    <chr>   <chr>   <int>
##  1 CAM     NO         52
##  2 CAM     YES         4
##  3 CHOW    NO          8
##  4 D       NO          1
##  5 D       YES         3
##  6 DCHE    NO         24
##  7 DCHE    YES        10
##  8 DPER    NO         56
##  9 DPER    YES        22
## 10 ECIR    NO         21
## 11 ECIR    YES        11
## 12 PSRUB   NO          4
## 13 PSRUB   YES         2
## 14 SAL     YES         1
## 15 <NA>    <NA>        2

Across all of our study sites, we had 24 captures of D. cheaha and 10 escapes.

points %>% filter(Occupied == 1 & Species == "DCHE") %>%
  group_by(StrmID, Escaped) %>%
  summarize(n())
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'StrmID'. You can override using the
## `.groups` argument.
## # A tibble: 11 × 3
## # Groups:   StrmID [7]
##    StrmID Escaped `n()`
##    <chr>  <chr>   <int>
##  1 STRM01 NO          5
##  2 STRM01 YES         2
##  3 STRM02 NO          5
##  4 STRM02 YES         1
##  5 STRM04 NO          2
##  6 STRM04 YES         1
##  7 STRM06 NO          1
##  8 STRM07 NO         11
##  9 STRM07 YES         2
## 10 STRM08 YES         3
## 11 STRM10 YES         1

When we look at a breakdown of escapes at each site, we find that we did not have any successful captures of D cheaha, at two of our sites which leaves our count of streams with successful captures at n = 5.

points %>% filter(Occupied ==1 & Species == "DCHE" & Escaped == "NO") %>%
  group_by(StrmID)%>%
  summarize(n())
## # A tibble: 5 × 2
##   StrmID `n()`
##   <chr>  <int>
## 1 STRM01     5
## 2 STRM02     5
## 3 STRM04     2
## 4 STRM06     1
## 5 STRM07    11
pointsDCHE = points %>% filter(Species == "DCHE")

pointsDCHE2 = pointsDCHE %>% filter(Escaped =="NO" & CoverType %in% c("R", "W")) %>%
  mutate(rocky = per_grav + per_rock)

Which size metrics to look at?

I have multiple size metrics: * Snout-vent length * total length * mass in grams * tail score

I expect that larger seal salamanders will occupy larger cover objects

habitat changes and salamander size

We also expect larger salamanders to occupy more aquatic habitat

FIGURE 8: LINEAR REGRESSION PLOT SHOWING SEAL SALAMANDER BODY SIZE VS COVER OBJECT AREA

lr = ggplot(pointsDCHE2, aes(x = covArea, y = mmSVL)) + 
  geom_smooth( method = "lm", col= "#5D695C", alpha = 0.5) + 
  geom_point(aes(col = hab),
             size = 2) + 
  scale_color_manual(values = habCols) + 
  labs(color = "") + 
  xlab(expression(paste("Cover Object Area (", cm^{2}, ")"))) + 
  ylab("Snout-Vent Length (mm)") +
  # labs(title = expression(
  #   paste(
  #     "Cover Object Area vs. ", 
  #     italic("Desmognathus cheaha"), 
  #     " mass"
  #     )
  #   ))+
  
  #facet_wrap(~hab) + 
  theme_bw() + 
  expand_limits(x = 2500) + 
theme(strip.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", color = "black"), 
      text = element_text(size = 20, family = "Times"), 
       panel.background = element_rect(fill='transparent'),
        plot.background = element_rect(fill='transparent', color=NA))


lr
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

From this figure, it does look like larger seal salamanders use more aquatic habitat than smaller ones. We will test for a significant relationship here:

habUseDCHE_null = glmmTMB(mmSVL ~ (1|StrmID), data = pointsDCHE2)



habUseDCHE = glmmTMB(mmSVL ~ hab + covArea + (1|StrmID), data = pointsDCHE2)

diagnose(habUseDCHE)
## Unusually large Z-statistics (|x|>5):
## 
##   (Intercept) d~(Intercept) 
##      10.37958      13.53059 
## 
## Large Z-statistics (estimate/std err) suggest a *possible* failure of
## the Wald approximation - often also associated with parameters that are
## at or near the edge of their range (e.g. random-effects standard
## deviations approaching 0).  (Alternately, they may simply represent
## very well-estimated parameters; intercepts of non-centered models may
## fall in this category.) While the Wald p-values and standard errors
## listed in summary() may be unreliable, profile confidence intervals
## (see ?confint.glmmTMB) and likelihood ratio test p-values derived by
## comparing models (e.g. ?drop1) are probably still OK.  (Note that the
## LRT is conservative when the null value is on the boundary, e.g. a
## variance or zero-inflation value of 0 (Self and Liang 1987; Stram and
## Lee 1994; Goldman and Whelan 2000); in simple cases these p-values are
## approximately twice as large as they should be.)
summary(habUseDCHE)
##  Family: gaussian  ( identity )
## Formula:          mmSVL ~ hab + covArea + (1 | StrmID)
## Data: pointsDCHE2
## 
##      AIC      BIC   logLik deviance df.resid 
##    175.7    181.2    -82.9    165.7       17 
## 
## Random effects:
## 
## Conditional model:
##  Groups   Name        Variance Std.Dev.
##  StrmID   (Intercept) 16.81    4.100   
##  Residual             97.40    9.869   
## Number of obs: 22, groups:  StrmID, 5
## 
## Dispersion estimate for gaussian family (sigma^2): 97.4 
## 
## Conditional model:
##              Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept) 44.952173   4.330828  10.380   <2e-16 ***
## habstream    7.578193   4.913133   1.542   0.1230    
## covArea      0.010513   0.004607   2.282   0.0225 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
tab_model(habUseDCHE)
  mmSVL
Predictors Estimates CI p
(Intercept) 44.95 36.46 – 53.44 <0.001
hab [stream] 7.58 -2.05 – 17.21 0.123
covArea 0.01 0.00 – 0.02 0.022
Random Effects
σ2 97.40
τ00 StrmID 16.81
ICC 0.15
N StrmID 5
Observations 22
Marginal R2 / Conditional R2 0.413 / 0.499 
habUseDCHE
## Formula:          mmSVL ~ hab + covArea + (1 | StrmID)
## Data: pointsDCHE2
##      AIC      BIC   logLik df.resid 
## 175.7322 181.1874 -82.8661       17 
## Random-effects (co)variances:
## 
## Conditional model:
##  Groups   Name        Std.Dev.
##  StrmID   (Intercept) 4.100   
##  Residual             9.869   
## 
## Number of obs: 22 / Conditional model: StrmID, 5
## 
## Dispersion estimate for gaussian family (sigma^2): 97.4 
## 
## Fixed Effects:
## 
## Conditional model:
## (Intercept)    habstream      covArea  
##    44.95217      7.57819      0.01051
anova(habUseDCHE_null, habUseDCHE, test = "Chisq")
## Data: pointsDCHE2
## Models:
## habUseDCHE_null: mmSVL ~ (1 | StrmID), zi=~0, disp=~1
## habUseDCHE: mmSVL ~ hab + covArea + (1 | StrmID), zi=~0, disp=~1
##                 Df    AIC    BIC  logLik deviance  Chisq Chi Df Pr(>Chisq)   
## habUseDCHE_null  3 183.25 186.52 -88.623   177.25                            
## habUseDCHE       5 175.73 181.19 -82.866   165.73 11.514      2    0.00316 **
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Are cover area and position autocorrelated?

sealStreams = c("STRM01", "STRM02", "STRM04", "STRM06", "STRM07", "STRM08", "STRM10")

pWS = pointsWide %>% filter(StrmID %in% sealStreams & CoverType %in% c("R", "W")) %>%  
  mutate(hab = ifelse(distWater > 50, "terrestrial", NA)) %>%
  mutate(hab = ifelse(cmDepth > 1, "stream", hab)) %>%
  mutate(hab = ifelse(is.na(hab) == T, "margin", hab)) %>%
  mutate(hab = as.factor(hab)) %>% filter(hab %in% c("stream", "margin"))

This is available in the text as Supplemental Table 4

habType = glmmTMB(covArea ~ hab + (1|StrmID), data = pWS)
## Warning in finalizeTMB(TMBStruc, obj, fit, h, data.tmb.old): Model convergence
## problem; non-positive-definite Hessian matrix. See vignette('troubleshooting')
confint(habType)
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(as.numeric(object$diag.cov.random)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(diag(object$cov.fixed)): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(as.numeric(object$diag.cov.random)): NaNs produced
##                                2.5 %   97.5 %    Estimate
## (Intercept)                232.49540 410.5622 321.5288091
## habstream                  -21.19014 209.1816  93.9957492
## Std.Dev.(Intercept)|StrmID       NaN      NaN   0.1401734
summary(habType)
##  Family: gaussian  ( identity )
## Formula:          covArea ~ hab + (1 | StrmID)
## Data: pWS
## 
##      AIC      BIC   logLik deviance df.resid 
##       NA       NA       NA       NA      232 
## 
## Random effects:
## 
## Conditional model:
##  Groups   Name        Variance  Std.Dev.
##  StrmID   (Intercept) 1.965e-02   0.1402
##  Residual             1.960e+05 442.7580
## Number of obs: 236, groups:  StrmID, 7
## 
## Dispersion estimate for gaussian family (sigma^2): 1.96e+05 
## 
## Conditional model:
##             Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)   321.53      45.43   7.078 1.46e-12 ***
## habstream      94.00      58.77   1.599     0.11    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
tab_model(habType)
  covArea
Predictors Estimates CI p
(Intercept) 321.53 232.50 – 410.56 <0.001
hab [stream] 94.00 -21.19 – 209.18 0.110
Random Effects
σ2 196034.64
τ00 StrmID 0.02
ICC 0.00
N StrmID 7
Observations 236
Marginal R2 / Conditional R2 0.011 / 0.011 
pWS %>% ggplot(aes(x = hab, y = covArea)) + geom_boxplot() + geom_jitter() + 
  theme_bw()

It does not look like there’s a difference in background points

sealPresLabs = c("0" = "Not Occupied by Seal Salamanders", 
                "1" = "Occupied by Seal Salamanders")



pWS %>% filter(hab %in% c("margin", "stream")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = hab, y = covArea)) + 
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) + 
  geom_jitter(aes(col = hab), width = 0.1, alpha = 0.5) + 
  scale_color_manual(values = habCols) + 
  ylab(expression(paste("Cover Object Area (", cm^{2}, ")"))) + 
  xlab("Habitat Type") + 
  labs(title = "Comparison of cover object size across habitat type", color = "") + 
  theme_bw() + 
  facet_wrap(~as.factor(sealPres), labeller = as_labeller(sealPresLabs)) + 
  theme(text = element_text(size = 12, family = "Times"), 
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", color = "black"))

plots %>% select(Date, PlotCode) %>% arrange(Date)

## 1  2023-03-17   STRM01
## 2  2023-03-18   STRM02
